# Problem Details Instructions (RFC 9457)

## Purpose

This file documents the patterns and requirements for implementing **RFC 9457 Problem Details** - the standard error response format for all APIs.

## File Patterns

**Applies to**: `**/common/problem-details.ts`, `**/exceptions/*.ts`, `**/filters/*.ts`

---

## RFC 9457 Overview

Problem Details provides a standardized way to carry machine-readable details of errors in HTTP responses.

```json
{
  "type": "https://problems.exprealty.com/validation-error",
  "title": "Validation failed",
  "status": 400,
  "detail": "The 'email' field contains an invalid email address",
  "instance": "/v1/agents/123",
  "traceId": "abc-123-def",
  "invalidParams": [
    { "name": "email", "reason": "Invalid email format", "in": "body" }
  ]
}
```

---

## Core Interface

```typescript
export interface ProblemDetails {
  /** A URI reference that identifies the problem type */
  type: string
  
  /** Short, human-readable summary of the problem type */
  title: string
  
  /** HTTP status code generated by the origin server */
  status: number
  
  /** Human-readable explanation specific to this occurrence */
  detail?: string
  
  /** A URI reference that identifies the specific occurrence */
  instance?: string

  /** eXp Extensions */
  traceId?: string              // Correlates with X-Request-Id
  invalidParams?: InvalidParam[] // For 400/422 validation errors

  // Additional RFC 9457 extension members allowed
  [ext: string]: unknown
}

export interface InvalidParam {
  /** JSON Pointer-ish path or dotted path of the invalid field */
  name: string
  
  /** Human-readable reason for the validation failure */
  reason: string
  
  /** Where the param came from (optional hint for clients) */
  in?: 'query' | 'path' | 'header' | 'cookie' | 'body'
}
```

---

## Problem Type URIs

### Canonical Base

```typescript
export const DEFAULT_PROBLEM_BASE = 
  process.env.PROBLEM_BASE?.replace(/\/+$/, '') || 
  'https://problems.exprealty.com'
```

### Creating Problem Type URIs

```typescript
export function makeProblemType(key: string): string {
  const base = DEFAULT_PROBLEM_BASE
  const k = key.replace(/^\/+/, '')
  return `${base}/${k}`
}

// Usage:
makeProblemType('validation-error')
// → 'https://problems.exprealty.com/validation-error'
```

---

## Standard Problem Keys

```typescript
export const ProblemKeys = {
  Validation: 'validation-error',
  NotFound: 'not-found',
  Unauthorized: 'unauthorized',
  Forbidden: 'forbidden',
  Conflict: 'conflict',
  RateLimited: 'rate-limited',
  BadGateway: 'bad-gateway',
  Upstream: 'upstream-error',
  Timeout: 'gateway-timeout',
  Internal: 'internal-error',
  ServiceUnavailable: 'service-unavailable',
} as const
```

### Pre-Built Problem Type URIs

```typescript
export const ProblemTypes = {
  Validation: makeProblemType(ProblemKeys.Validation),
  NotFound: makeProblemType(ProblemKeys.NotFound),
  Unauthorized: makeProblemType(ProblemKeys.Unauthorized),
  Forbidden: makeProblemType(ProblemKeys.Forbidden),
  Conflict: makeProblemType(ProblemKeys.Conflict),
  RateLimited: makeProblemType(ProblemKeys.RateLimited),
  BadGateway: makeProblemType(ProblemKeys.BadGateway),
  Upstream: makeProblemType(ProblemKeys.Upstream),
  Timeout: makeProblemType(ProblemKeys.Timeout),
  Internal: makeProblemType(ProblemKeys.Internal),
  ServiceUnavailable: makeProblemType(ProblemKeys.ServiceUnavailable),
} as const
```

---

## Default Titles

```typescript
export const ProblemTitles: Record<string, string> = {
  [ProblemTypes.Validation]: 'Validation failed',
  [ProblemTypes.NotFound]: 'Not Found',
  [ProblemTypes.Unauthorized]: 'Unauthorized',
  [ProblemTypes.Forbidden]: 'Forbidden',
  [ProblemTypes.Conflict]: 'Conflict',
  [ProblemTypes.RateLimited]: 'Too Many Requests',
  [ProblemTypes.BadGateway]: 'Bad Gateway',
  [ProblemTypes.Upstream]: 'Upstream Error',
  [ProblemTypes.Timeout]: 'Gateway Timeout',
  [ProblemTypes.Internal]: 'Internal Server Error',
  [ProblemTypes.ServiceUnavailable]: 'Service Unavailable',
}
```

---

## Creating Problem Details

### Generic Factory

```typescript
export function createProblem(opts: {
  type: string
  status: number
  title?: string
  detail?: string
  instance?: string
  traceId?: string
  invalidParams?: InvalidParam[]
  extensions?: Record<string, unknown>
}): ProblemDetails {
  const title = opts.title ?? ProblemTitles[opts.type]
  const pd: ProblemDetails = {
    type: opts.type,
    title,
    status: opts.status,
    detail: opts.detail,
    instance: opts.instance,
    traceId: opts.traceId,
  }
  if (opts.invalidParams?.length) {
    pd.invalidParams = opts.invalidParams
  }
  if (opts.extensions) {
    Object.assign(pd, opts.extensions)
  }
  return pd
}
```

### Convenience Creators

```typescript
export const Problems = {
  validation(
    detail: string,
    invalidParams?: InvalidParam[],
    instance?: string,
    traceId?: string,
  ): ProblemDetails {
    return createProblem({
      type: ProblemTypes.Validation,
      status: 400,
      detail,
      instance,
      traceId,
      invalidParams,
    })
  },

  notFound(
    detail = 'No resource matched the request path.',
    instance?: string,
    traceId?: string,
  ): ProblemDetails {
    return createProblem({
      type: ProblemTypes.NotFound,
      status: 404,
      detail,
      instance,
      traceId,
    })
  },

  unauthorized(
    detail = 'Authentication required.',
    instance?: string,
    traceId?: string,
  ): ProblemDetails { ... },

  forbidden(
    detail = 'You do not have permission to perform this action.',
    instance?: string,
    traceId?: string,
  ): ProblemDetails { ... },

  conflict(
    detail: string,
    instance?: string,
    traceId?: string,
  ): ProblemDetails { ... },

  internal(
    detail = 'An unexpected error occurred.',
    instance?: string,
    traceId?: string,
  ): ProblemDetails { ... },
}
```

---

## Requirements

### MUST Follow

1. **Always include `type`, `title`, and `status`**:
   ```typescript
   {
     type: ProblemTypes.NotFound,    // ✅ Required
     title: 'Not Found',              // ✅ Required
     status: 404,                     // ✅ Required
   }
   ```

2. **Always use canonical problem type URIs**:
   ```typescript
   type: ProblemTypes.Validation     // ✅ Uses standard
   type: 'validation-error'          // ❌ Not a URI
   ```

3. **Always include `traceId`** when available (from X-Request-Id)

4. **Always use `invalidParams`** for validation errors:
   ```typescript
   Problems.validation(
     'One or more fields are invalid',
     [
       { name: 'email', reason: 'Invalid email format', in: 'body' },
       { name: 'limit', reason: 'Must be between 1 and 50', in: 'query' },
     ]
   )
   ```

5. **Always set Content-Type header**:
   ```typescript
   res.setHeader('Content-Type', 'application/problem+json')
   ```

### MUST NOT Do

1. **Never include stack traces** in production responses
2. **Never expose internal implementation details** in `detail`
3. **Never use custom problem types** without adding to `ProblemKeys`
4. **Never return non-RFC 9457 format** for errors

---

## Mapping HTTP Status to Problem Type

| Status | Problem Type | Use Case |
|--------|--------------|----------|
| 400 | `Validation` | Invalid input, malformed request |
| 401 | `Unauthorized` | Missing/invalid authentication |
| 403 | `Forbidden` | Authenticated but not authorized |
| 404 | `NotFound` | Resource doesn't exist |
| 409 | `Conflict` | Duplicate, constraint violation |
| 429 | `RateLimited` | Too many requests |
| 500 | `Internal` | Unexpected server error |
| 502 | `BadGateway` | Upstream service failure |
| 503 | `ServiceUnavailable` | Service temporarily unavailable |
| 504 | `Timeout` | Upstream timeout |

---

## Usage in NestJS Exception Filter

```typescript
import { 
  ProblemTypes, 
  ProblemTitles, 
  createProblem, 
  Problems 
} from '@exprealty/shared-domain'

@Catch()
export class ProblemDetailsFilter implements ExceptionFilter {
  catch(exception: unknown, host: ArgumentsHost) {
    const ctx = host.switchToHttp()
    const response = ctx.getResponse<Response>()
    const request = ctx.getRequest<Request>()
    const traceId = request.headers['x-request-id'] as string

    let problem: ProblemDetails

    if (exception instanceof NotFoundException) {
      problem = Problems.notFound(
        exception.message,
        request.url,
        traceId,
      )
    } else if (exception instanceof BadRequestException) {
      // Handle validation errors
      const response = exception.getResponse() as any
      problem = Problems.validation(
        response.message || 'Validation failed',
        response.invalidParams,
        request.url,
        traceId,
      )
    } else {
      problem = Problems.internal(
        'An unexpected error occurred',
        request.url,
        traceId,
      )
    }

    response
      .status(problem.status)
      .setHeader('Content-Type', 'application/problem+json')
      .json(problem)
  }
}
```

---

## Zod Integration

Convert Zod errors to invalidParams:

```typescript
function zodErrorToInvalidParams(error: z.ZodError): InvalidParam[] {
  return error.issues.map((issue) => ({
    name: issue.path.join('.'),
    reason: issue.message,
    in: 'body' as const,
  }))
}

// Usage in validation pipe
try {
  return schema.parse(value)
} catch (error) {
  if (error instanceof z.ZodError) {
    throw new BadRequestException({
      message: 'Validation failed',
      invalidParams: zodErrorToInvalidParams(error),
    })
  }
  throw error
}
```

---

## Testing Requirements

```typescript
describe('Problems', () => {
  describe('validation', () => {
    it('should create validation problem with correct structure', () => {
      const problem = Problems.validation(
        'Invalid input',
        [{ name: 'email', reason: 'Invalid format', in: 'body' }],
        '/v1/agents',
        'trace-123',
      )

      expect(problem).toEqual({
        type: 'https://problems.exprealty.com/validation-error',
        title: 'Validation failed',
        status: 400,
        detail: 'Invalid input',
        instance: '/v1/agents',
        traceId: 'trace-123',
        invalidParams: [
          { name: 'email', reason: 'Invalid format', in: 'body' }
        ],
      })
    })
  })

  describe('notFound', () => {
    it('should create 404 problem', () => {
      const problem = Problems.notFound('Agent not found')
      expect(problem.status).toBe(404)
      expect(problem.type).toBe(ProblemTypes.NotFound)
    })
  })
})

describe('makeProblemType', () => {
  it('should create canonical URI', () => {
    expect(makeProblemType('validation-error'))
      .toBe('https://problems.exprealty.com/validation-error')
  })

  it('should strip leading slashes', () => {
    expect(makeProblemType('/not-found'))
      .toBe('https://problems.exprealty.com/not-found')
  })
})
```
